summary: Russia pursues a sovereign track through TC26, the Rosstandart Technical Committee for Standardization Cryptographic Information Protection, whose Working Group 2.5 is developing post-quantum cryptographic mechanisms to extend the national GOST R 34 cryptographic series. No adopted national post-quantum standard had been published as of June 2026.
govActors: TC26 (Rosstandart) | Technical Committee for Standardization Cryptographic Information Protection, whose Working Group 2.5 develops post-quantum cryptographic mechanisms to extend the GOST R 34 series
provenance: CAVEAT: every RUS result is included:false, so under a strict reading this country would be a Placeholder. Profile drafted from T1 institutional candidate material (the official TC26 / Rosstandart site) because it clearly attests a sovereign GOST posture and the map needs a colour - confirm inclusion before treating as settled - REVIEW

dominantProcess Sovereign + standardFamilies (GOST R 34 series) + govActors: TC26 structure page listing Working Group 2.5 Post-Quantum Cryptographic Mechanisms under Subcommittee No. 2, the TC26 about page, and the TC26 standardization-documents index (all tc26.ru, T1)
no adopted PQC standard: the TC26 draft-documents page shows no post-quantum-specific draft published as of June 2026

algorithms OMITTED: no GOST PQC algorithm is named in an institutional source; the Shipovnik code-based signature appears only in vendor (Kryptonite) and aggregator (TAdviser) candidates, excluded per the iron rule
hybridDeployment / migrationTimeline / targetCompletion OMITTED: none stated; financial-sector pilots (Gazprombank, Moscow Exchange, NSPK) appear only in non-institutional TAdviser candidates
National Technology Center for Digital Cryptography (Mintsifry) is reported to host a post-quantum laboratory but no explicit PQC document was found on its site; not relied on
